projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

needs_attention.title: Contact installers for quotes

insights.summary: The consistent 8-10 device count alongside zero camera motion at 9:42 PM on a school night strongly indicates the household is settled for the evening, likely with everyone in bedrooms or a dimly lit common area, confirming the flagged pattern is benign network behavior rather than an anomaly.

The consistent presence of 9-10 mobile devices alongside zero camera motion for four hours indicates the entire household is gathered in a single room, likely the living area, for a shared evening activity.

The persistent 8-10 device count alongside zero camera motion for four hours strongly suggests either phones are stationary (e.g., charging) or the family is gathered in a camera blind spot; a quick verification of camera status and a check-in is recommended to rule out a system issue.

The consistent 8-9 device presence alongside zero camera motion for four hours strongly suggests the household is congregating in a single camera-blind zone or the presence sensors are reporting stale/cached data rather than active movement. A quick verification of camera coverage or sensor health is recommended.

The consistent presence of 8-9 devices combined with zero camera motion for four hours on a Monday afternoon strongly suggests the household is home but stationary, likely due to remote work, school, or a quiet day; the complete lack of movement from active dogs warrants a quick check to rule out camera obstruction or malfunction.

The steady presence of 7-9 devices alongside zero camera motion for four hours on a Monday afternoon strongly indicates the house is unoccupied, as the family and active dogs are likely away despite their devices remaining connected.

The combination of 7-10 active devices at home on a Monday afternoon with zero camera motion for four hours strongly suggests the household is gathered in a non-camera zone or the cameras are blind; given two active dogs and two kids are home, this warrants a quick verification that the cameras are functioning and everyone is safe.

The steady decline in active mobile devices (10 down to 7) alongside zero camera motion for four hours indicates a worsening network or router issue causing devices to drop offline, rather than normal household movement, which is highly unusual for a Monday afternoon with the family expected home.

The consistent presence of 9-10 devices alongside zero camera motion for four hours on a Monday midday indicates the entire household is likely home but completely inactive, which is highly atypical for a school/work day and suggests possible illness, a planned day off, or a presence sensor glitch that needs verification.

The persistent 9-10 device count combined with zero camera motion for four hours on a Monday morning confirms the home is unoccupied, indicating the presence signal is a false positive from devices left behind or network caching. This matters because it validates the family is at work or school, allowing the system to suppress false occupancy alerts and adjust automations accordingly.
